MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-17-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Brian A. Fiorino, Esq., of HOWARD ROME MARTIN & RIDLEY LLP (650-365-7715, X233), appearing for defendant / moving party IMO Industries, Inc.
Ksenia L. Snylyk, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Wayne Hartman

RULING:
DEFENDANT IMO INDUSTRIES INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant IMO Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability) and taken off calendar as to issues 3 and 4 (false representation and punitive damages). Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets attributable to defendant. Pages 376-386 of plaintiff's deposition transcript preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar with regard to plaintiff's exposure to asbestos-containing gaskets attributable to defendant. Defendant's separate statement does not include facts sufficient to establish that it is likely to prevail on its defense of sophisticated user/intermediary. There are no facts regarding either the plaintiff's or the Navy's knowledge or constructive knowledge of asbestos-containing gaskets. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for false representation and punitive damages. Overruled as to all objections to the Hartman declaration. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
